summary.intent: Address three Codex review findings on PR #724 in the ars-ui repo: propagate fieldset error-driven invalid state to descendant fields (Leptos), refresh form root attrs on prop-only rerenders (Dioxus), and sync invalid native submit controls into named field validation errors (both adapters).
summary.outcome: All three review findings were fixed with targeted adapter changes and regression tests, formatting and clippy passed, wasm targets compiled cleanly, and the fixes were committed as 'Address form validation review' with a Codex co-author trailer.

repo: Dioxus already derived inherited fieldset invalid state from the machine; the missing propagation was Leptos-only.
Leptos exposes `web_sys` and `wasm_bindgen::JsCast` through the `leptos` crate itself, so no new `web-sys` dependency entry is needed for DOM inspection in ars-leptos.
The repo uses `cargo xtask ci fmt` and `cargo xclippy` as the CI gate commands; `cargo +nightly fmt --all` is the local apply command.

Browser wasm tests require `WASM_BINDGEN_TEST_ONLY_WEB=1` and a local server the sandbox cannot spawn; wasm compile checks (`cargo +nightly check --tests --target wasm32-unknown-unknown`) are the viable local proxy.
The project uses a Codex bot that monitors PRs via GitHub reactions (`eyes` → review in progress, reaction drops + threads appear on completion) and is triggered by `@codex review` PR comments.
Dioxus `dx fmt` can reintroduce malformed `rsx!` fragments when a call is line-broken inside `rsx!`; restructuring to a single-line call or extracting to a local variable avoids the formatter bug.
The `unused_qualifications` lint fires as a false positive on Dioxus event attributes in `rsx!`; a targeted `#[expect]` is the accepted pattern.

code.finding: Added logic to derive inherited invalid state from fieldset machine errors, mirroring the explicit `invalid=true` propagation path.

Added browser-only code to query invalid named native controls on blocked ARIA submit, convert browser `validationMessage()` to `ars_forms::validation::Error`, and dispatch `SetValidationErrors` before updating form status.

Fixed stale root-attrs memo by reading machine state/context signals explicitly before taking the snapshot, ensuring prop rerenders and machine changes both recompute DOM attrs. Also added same invalid-native-submit sync as Leptos.

Formatter-stable pattern: store `form_ref()` in a local variable before calling `invalid_control_errors`; inline line-broken calls inside `rsx!` trigger a `dx fmt` bug that reinserts malformed fragments.

workflow: Poll Codex review state via GitHub reactions API (`eyes` reaction = in progress) and GraphQL unresolved-thread count rather than waiting for email notifications.
When a commit hook takes >60s with no output, check the process tree before assuming stuck; sandbox restrictions may block `ps` inspection.
Run `git status --short` and `git diff --stat` before committing to confirm only intended files are staged.
For wasm-only browser paths that can't run locally (sandbox server-spawn restriction), wasm compile checks are sufficient to gate the PR; note the limitation explicitly in the session.

friction: Git commit hook ran for over a minute with no output, causing repeated stalled waits and uncertainty about whether the commit was proceeding or stuck.
First `gh pr comment` attempt to retrigger Codex failed with a network connection error and required a retry.

`dx fmt` (Dioxus formatter) reintroduced a malformed `rsx!` fragment after every format run when the `invalid_control_errors` call was line-broken inside `rsx!`, requiring a formatter-stable workaround.

Browser wasm tests cannot spawn their local server in the sandbox (`Operation not permitted`), so the new browser-only behavior (invalid native submit sync) could only be verified via wasm compile checks, not actual test execution.
Process inspection via `ps` was blocked by the sandbox, preventing diagnosis of the slow commit hook.
open_items: The invalid native-submit sync populates `FormContext` validation errors by control name, but low-level `Field` components have no registration path from DOM constraints back to their own machines; a proper `TextField`-level registration system would be needed for full round-trip error display without relying on the `name` key match.

Wasm browser tests for the three new regression cases (`form_invalid_required_submit_updates_named_field_errors`, `fieldset_errors_reach_descendant_field_invalid_attrs`, Dioxus form action/role refresh) were added but never actually executed — only compile-checked — due to sandbox server-spawn restrictions.
A known external `block v0.1.6` future-incompat note surfaces in `cargo xclippy` output; this is unrelated to the PR changes but has not been resolved.
